title: Gateless Gate
notes: 48 koans with Wumen's verse and commentary. The title koan (Case 1, Zhaozhou's 無) is the primary entry point into Rinzai practice.

title: Blue Cliff Record
notes: 100 koans with verse and prose commentary. More literary and philosophically elaborate than the Gateless Gate. Essential for advanced Rinzai study.

title: Platform Sutra of the Sixth Patriarch
period: Attributed to Huineng (638–713 CE); compiled in current form c. 8th–9th century
notes: The foundational text of the Southern school of Chan. Huineng's teaching on sudden awakening and the originally pure nature of mind. The only Chinese text accorded the status of 'sutra.'

title: Heart Sutra
period: Sanskrit original c. 1st–2nd century CE; Xuanzang's Chinese translation 649 CE
notes: The most recited text in East Asian Buddhism. 260 characters in Xuanzang's version. The 色即是空 passage is the doctrinal center of the Prajnaparamita corpus.

title: Hyakuichi Shinron
notes: The text in which Nishi Amane first published several of his philosophical coinages including 哲学. Represents the systematic effort to render Western philosophical vocabulary in Chinese characters.

title: An Encouragement of Learning
notes: The most widely read Meiji text. Establishes the conceptual ground for liberal political vocabulary. The opening line on human equality became iconic.

title: Xinmin Shuo (On the New Citizen)
notes: Liang Qichao's major work written during his exile in Japan. The primary vector through which wasei-kango vocabulary entered Chinese intellectual discourse. Systematically deploys Japanese coinages as naturalized Chinese terms.

title: Impressions from Travels in Europe
notes: Written after Liang's visit to post-WWI Europe. Marks a turn away from uncritical faith in Western science and democracy. Key text for showing the imported vocabulary being questioned by a major Chinese thinker.
